script.dialogue: Of all the people to be assigned chore duty with…
Can’t say I’m particularly happy about it either. Let’s just do our best to get these books re-shelved, alright?
Some time later…
Okay. This stack goes to the history aisle, those magic tomes go in the reason aisle, and these novels belong in the fiction aisle.
Excellent. I’ll handle the tomes.
Some time later…
You’ve been back here a while. What are you doing?
Ah, sorry—I happened to notice this shelf was sorted incorrectly. Figured I’d take care of it while I was in the neighborhood.
…I see. Carry on, then.
Some time later…
And we’re done! In record time, too.
Indeed. It would seem you’re more meticulous than I’d originally thought.
Uh, thanks, I think? I just like keeping my spaces ordered. Plus, the faster I am at cleaning, the more time I have to take a pretty girl out on a date.
Of course. I suppose the diligence is admirable, even if the motivation is unsavory.
Yeah, yeah, we all know how little you care for me. Doesn’t that curmudgeon routine ever get old?
Hardly. It keeps frivolous individuals from approaching me—most of them, anyway. The fewer people are yapping away in my ear, the fewer distractions there are to interrupt my tasks.
Guess we each have our own way of doing things that works for us.
Anyway! Since we finished early, how about we play a game?
What about anything I’ve just said makes you think—wait, is that a chess set?
Sure is. I found it while I was reorganizing the storage closet.
We… weren’t asked to reorganize the storage closet.
Yet it clearly needed a little tidying up. I’m sure the librarian will thank me later. Do you want to play or not? I see you and Edelgard dueling each other in the classroom all the time.
…I suppose one game won’t hurt.
Some time later…
Your move.
What a fascinating gambit…
Some time later…
…Checkmate.
Ha! You got me. That was a great game. Thanks for—
Hold, Gautier.
Hm? Have I managed to offend you again, Hubert?
In a sense. I believe you threw that game.
Interesting theory. You think I’d give up an opportunity to beat you at something?

Not initially. You were playing quite well at the beginning. Anticipating my moves, keeping me guessing. Then, suddenly, you began making mistakes. Major ones that I wouldn’t have expected based on what I’d seen.
Maybe you’re just that good.
Don’t patronize me. Why would it even occur to you to do that?
Okay, fine, yes. I threw the game—but I didn’t go in planning to. I thought it would be fun to surprise you again, since you seem to have such a low opinion of me. Then I got nervous, because…
Go on.
I don’t know. Maybe I decided it would be better if you went on thinking I’m a good-for-nothing slacker. You seemed happy enough with that.
What utter nonsense. It doesn’t make me happy to see one as competent as you not live up to your potential.
The world has enough truly irredeemable nobles running around, misusing power they’ve done nothing to earn. We don’t need the small handful of upstanding ones worsening the situation.
I, uh… Okay. I see your point. I’m not sure if “upstanding” is the best word to describe me, but… Guess I owe you a proper chess game now, huh?
Another day, perhaps. I suspect you have responsibilities to shirk and women to irritate.
And I’m sure you have a whole laundry list of errands to run yourself ragged with.
Heh. Until next time, then.
Sylvain and Hubert have reached Support Rank B.
